253:     always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)
254:     begin
255:         if(~trstn_i)
256:             internal_reg_error = 33'h0;
257:         else if(intreg_ld_en && (reg_select_data == `DBG_LINT_INTREG_ERROR))  // do load from data input register
258:         begin
259:             if(data_register_i[46])
260:                 internal_reg_error[0] = 1'b0;  // if write data is 1, reset the error bit
261:         end
262:         else if(error_reg_en && !internal_reg_error[0])
263:         begin
264:             if(biu_err || (!biu_ready))  internal_reg_error[0] = 1'b1;
265:              else if(biu_strobe) internal_reg_error[32:1] = address_counter;
266:         end
267:         else if(biu_strobe && !internal_reg_error[0])
268:             internal_reg_error[32:1] = address_counter;  // When no error, latch this whether error_reg_en or not
269:         end
270: 
271:    ///////////////////////////////////////////////
272:    // Address counter
273: 
274:    assign data_to_addr_counter = (addr_sel) ? incremented_address[31:0] : address_data_in;
275:    assign incremented_address = address_counter + word_size_bytes;
276: 
277:    // Technically, since this data (sometimes) comes from the input shift reg, we should latch on
278:    // negedge, per the JTAG spec. But that makes things difficult when incrementing.
279:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)  // JTAG spec specifies latch on negative edge in UPDATE_DR state
280:      begin
281:     if(~trstn_i)
282:       address_counter <= 32'h0;
283:     else if(addr_ct_en)
284:       address_counter <= data_to_addr_counter;
285:      end
286:

289: 
290:    always @ (posedge tck_i or negedge trstn_i)  // JTAG spec specifies latch on negative edge in UPDATE_DR state
291:      begin

294:     else if(op_reg_en)

307: 
308:      end

311:    assign bit_count_32 = (bit_count == 6'h20) ? 1'b1 : 1'b0;
312:
